release_review.summary: Runtime security boundaries, published Flowersec and Floe Webapp dependencies, Flower and Floret integration, Desktop lifecycle handling, and Env App product behavior are refreshed without changing the Runtime Service compatibility window.
checked_surfaces: Runtime Service protocol_version remains redeven-runtime-v1; the compatibility epoch remains 5 and minimum Desktop/Runtime versions remain v0.7.0

Remote Flowersec connections explicitly require TLS while direct Local UI transport is restricted to literal loopback authorities; Host and Origin validation, HTTP limits, WebSocket limits, and security headers do not alter Runtime Service request or response schemas

Startup passwords and bootstrap tickets use prompt, stdin, protected file, fixed environment, or Desktop envelope ingress; removed secret-bearing flags and encrypted runtime persistence change command security without changing Desktop attach/start or structured startup report fields

Shell and process launch now require both write and execute permission across terminal, Flower, Floret, Codex bridge, Desktop, Local UI, and remote sessions; execute_read remains limited to explicitly modeled non-mutating operations

Flowersec Go and TypeScript capabilities are consumed from published v0.19.11 artifacts and Floe Webapp packages from published v0.36.74 artifacts, with no replace, go.work, package-manager link, or local sibling path wiring

Floret is consumed only from the published github.com/floegence/floret v0.3.90 module; projection recovery, independent token budgets, deletion fencing, and shared activity rebuilding remain internal to Flower and thread persistence

Desktop managed-process reconciliation, Env App activity and terminal recovery, plugin surfaces, settings, and navigation behavior preserve the existing Runtime Service control methods and gateway lifecycle payloads

Desktop attach/start, Local UI health, structured startup reports, sys.ping, Env App maintenance UI, gateway lifecycle, SSH runtime bootstrap, and installer version discovery were reviewed against the existing v0.7.0 minimum compatibility window
release_review.same_window_rationale: The compatibility window is unchanged because the release hardens local transport, credential ingress and persistence, process permission enforcement, and product lifecycle behavior without changing the redeven-runtime-v1 wire protocol. Published Flowersec v0.19.11, Floe Webapp v0.36.74, and Floret v0.3.90 integrations preserve Runtime Service request and response fields, control methods, attach/start handshakes, structured startup reports, sys.ping semantics, Local UI health, Env App maintenance routes, gateway lifecycle messages, SSH runtime bootstrap, and installer version discovery. Existing v0.7.0 Desktop and Runtime binaries therefore remain within the declared compatibility window.
